cluster_identifier.description: The Cluster Identifier. Must be a lower case string.

node_type.description: The node type to be provisioned for the cluster.

database_name.description: The name of the first database to be created when the cluster is created.

master_username.description: Username for the master DB user.

vpc_security_group_ids.description: A list of Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) security groups to be associated with the cluster.

availability_zone.description: The EC2 Availability Zone (AZ) in which you want Amazon Redshift to provision the cluster. For example, if you have several EC2 instances running in a specific Availability Zone, then you might want the cluster to be provisioned in the same zone in order to decrease network latency.

availability_zone_relocation_enabled.description: The cluster can be relocated to another availabity zone, either automatically by AWS or when requested. Available for use on clusters from the RA3 instance family.

preferred_maintenance_window.description: The weekly time range (in UTC) during which automated cluster maintenance can occur. Format: ddd:hh24:mi-ddd:hh24:mi

automated_snapshot_retention_period.description: The number of days that automated snapshots are retained. If the value is 0, automated snapshots are disabled. Even if automated snapshots are disabled, you can still create manual snapshots when you want with create-cluster-snapshot.

port.description: The port number on which the cluster accepts incoming connections. Valid values are between 1115 and 65535. The cluster is accessible only via the JDBC and ODBC connection strings. Part of the connection string requires the port on which the cluster will listen for incoming connections.

cluster_version.description: The version of the Amazon Redshift engine software that you want to deploy on the cluster. The version selected runs on all the nodes in the cluster.

allow_version_upgrade.description: If true , major version upgrades can be applied during the maintenance window to the Amazon Redshift engine that is running on the cluster.

apply_immediately.description: Specifies whether any cluster modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window.

number_of_nodes.description: The number of compute nodes in the cluster. This parameter is required when the ClusterType parameter is specified as multi-node.

encrypted.description: Whether to encrypt data in the cluster at rest.

elastic_ip.description: The Elastic IP (EIP) address for the cluster.

skip_final_snapshot.description: Determines whether a final snapshot of the cluster is created before Amazon Redshift deletes the cluster. If true , a final cluster snapshot is not created. If false , a final cluster snapshot is created before the cluster is deleted.

final_snapshot_identifier.description: The identifier of the final snapshot that is to be created immediately before deleting the cluster. If this parameter is provided, skip_final_snapshot must be false.

maintenance_track_name.description: The name of the maintenance track for the restored cluster. When you take a snapshot, the snapshot inherits the MaintenanceTrack value from the cluster.

manual_snapshot_retention_period.description: The default number of days to retain a manual snapshot. If the value is -1, the snapshot is retained indefinitely. This setting doesn't change the retention period of existing snapshots. Valid values are between -1 and 3653.

iam_roles.description: A list of IAM Role ARNs to associate with the cluster. A Maximum of 10 can be associated to the cluster at any time.

cluster_subnet_group_name.description: The name of a cluster subnet group to be associated with this cluster. If this parameter is not provided the resulting cluster will be deployed outside virtual private cloud (VPC).

master_password_secret_kms_key_id.description: ID of the KMS key used to encrypt the cluster admin credentials secret.

manage_master_password.description: Whether to use AWS SecretsManager to manage the cluster admin credentials.

publicly_accessible.description: Whether the cluster is publicly accessible.

kms_key_id.description: value of the KMS key used to encrypt the cluster.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource.
